page.content: Understanding the prevalence and impact of alcohol consumption within various demographics is crucial for informing public health initiatives and policy development. The data presented in the table illustrates the distribution of alcohol usage across distinct population groups, highlighting both the number of individuals (N) and the corresponding percentage (%) engaged in different levels of consumption. Of particular interest is the differentiation between moderate and heavy drinkers, as reflected in the columns labeled "N" and "%".

page.content: These metrics provide insight into not only the sheer volume of individuals consuming alcohol but also the proportion of the population that engages in potentially harmful drinking behaviors. Furthermore, the inclusion of the column labeled "P" allows for an assessment of the relationship between alcohol consumption patterns and other pertinent variables, such as age, gender, or socioeconomic status. By analyzing these data points, stakeholders can better understand the underlying trends and factors that contribute to alcohol use, thereby enabling more targeted interventions aimed at reducing alcohol-related harm in the community. This data serves as a foundational element in shaping effective educational campaigns and resource allocation for alcohol-related health services.
